extra.message: User input from data['name'] is concatenated directly into SQL string using string formatting (line 43: q = ("INSERT INTO students (name) VALUES ('%(name)s')" % {'name': name})). This allows SQL injection attacks to execute arbitrary SQL commands.
review.review_text is output without escaping in the template. Since Jinja2 autoescape is disabled in app.py:35, user-supplied review text is directly rendered as HTML, allowing stored XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Password hashes are compared using MD5 (line 41: return self.pwd_hash == md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()). MD5 is cryptographically broken and should not be used for password hashing. Use bcrypt, scrypt, Argon2, or PBKDF2 instead.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: Jinja2 template autoescape is explicitly disabled (autoescape=False on line 35). This disables HTML escaping by default for all templates, making the application vulnerable to stored and reflected XSS attacks when user input is rendered in templates without explicit escaping.
